Article content: {"title":"I Took All 20 Anthropic Courses. Here's the Only Tier List You Need. ","entities":{"code":[{"language":"none","code":"THE MAP: 20 ANTHROPIC COURSES, RANKED

C   Students · Educators · Nonprofits · Teaching · Capabilities   your role
D   MCP Advanced · Bedrock · Vertex                               only your stack

START   Claude 101, then Framework, then Cowork  (one weekend)
SKIP    Bedrock/Vertex unless that's your cloud; Capabilities if you're not new
CERT    16 of 20 (all but Builders, Platform 101, Agent Skills, Subagents)","content":"```

```"}],"mentions":[{"start":121,"end":131,"username":"phosphenq"}],"urls":[{"text":"anthropic.skilljar.com"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-platform-101"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-code-101"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-for-students"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-capabilities-and-limitations"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-101"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-model-context-protocol"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-for-builders"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/teaching-ai-fluency"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-with-google-vertex"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-with-the-anthropic-api"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/model-context-protocol-advanced-topics"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-agent-skills"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-framework-foundations"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-for-small-businesses"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-for-nonprofits"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-claude-cowork"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-101"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-subagents"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-in-amazon-bedrock"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-for-educators"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/ai-fluency-framework-foundations"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-code-in-action"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/"},{"text":"https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-claude-cowork"}]},"plain_text":"Most people open Claude, ask it one thing, skim the reply, and close the tab.
That's the whole relationship. They're running the most capable tool they've ever had at maybe a tenth of what it can do, and they have no idea what they're missing.
Meanwhile there's a whole industry charging $200, $500, sometimes two grand to teach the other ninety percent: prompt engineering, agents, building on the API, "AI for your business."
What nobody seems to mention is that Anthropic already teaches all of it. For free, on their own site, with a certificate at the end.
It's called Anthropic Academy. There are 20 courses on it, and I'd put money on almost nobody you follow having finished a single one.
I did all 20, every lesson and every quiz. They aren't thrown-together marketing either: Anthropic built them with university researchers and with companies like PayPal, CodePath, and GivingTuesday.
I'm Phosphen. I write long-form breakdowns of how the best AI labs actually build, and how to use what they ship. Follow @phosphenq, my DMs are open.
 
So I ranked them, S down to D.
One thing before you start: the order matters more than the courses themselves. Get it wrong and you'll quit one tier before the part that changes how you work.
If you only have one free weekend, do these three in order: Claude 101 to fix the basics, then AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations for the mental model, then Introduction to Claude Cowork to point it all at your real files.
By Sunday you'll be getting more out of Claude than almost everyone posting about it. Everything else here just stacks on top.
How the tiers work: S is for everyone, taken in order. Below that, jump to your lane. Signing up takes thirty seconds and costs nothing, just an email.
S-Tier: must-have
> Claude 101
You've used Claude plenty, but nobody ever actually showed you how. That gap is what this closes, in a single afternoon.
It's less about features than judgment: what to hand to the model, and what to keep for yourself.
Along the way it walks you through all three sides of Claude (chat, Cowork, and Code) and points out what you've been scrolling past for months.
Fourteen short lessons, no account needed to start. Once it clicks, the rest of this list gets easier.
> AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations
The one that quietly turns "I use AI" into "I'm actually good at AI."
It hands you a real mental model, the 4D Framework: how to decide what to delegate, how to describe it well, how to tell a sharp answer from a confident-sounding wrong one, and how to stay responsible for the result.
Two university professors built it with Anthropic, and almost every other course here assumes you've already done it.
Take it second.
> Introduction to Claude Cowork
Chat is the demo. This is the actual product.
Cowork is Claude working directly on your files, reading them, editing them, producing real output, right inside Chrome, Word, Excel, and Outlook.
Half an hour of video takes you from your first launch to running multi-step work without hovering over it the whole time.
The first time you watch it finish something you'd been dreading, it changes what you think the tool is for.
 
A-Tier: pick your lane
> Claude Code 101
The most powerful thing in this whole catalog. The misconception that costs people the most is thinking it's only for programmers. It isn't.
Claude Code is an agent that runs on your machine and actually does things: it reads your files, runs your commands, and carries out the multi-step work you'd normally grind through by hand.
Pointed at code, it's ridiculous. Pointed at research, spreadsheets, data, or ops, it's the closest thing I've found to untying your hands for anything you can describe.
Ninety minutes to set up, including the trick most people miss: keeping its context clean with /compact and /clear so it stays sharp on long sessions.
If your work touches a computer at all, this is the highest-leverage course on the list.
 
> AI Fluency for Small Business
The "AI for your business" course you keep seeing sold for a few hundred dollars, except it's free and built with real businesses, everything from PayPal down to a neighborhood butcher shop.
It takes the 4D Framework and aims it at what actually eats your week: customer messages, back-office work, operations.
No speeches about the future of work. Just where to put AI when you have no spare time and no spare staff.
> AI Fluency for Builders
Shipping fast with AI is the easy part. Shipping fast without quietly wrecking your own judgment is the hard part, and that's the whole course.
Built with CodePath, it's about knowing where to lean on the model and where to slow down and own the call, across the full path from a problem to something live.
One catch: it's the only AI Fluency course with no certificate. Take it for the skill, not the badge.
> Claude Platform 101
Most courses give you the SDK and call that teaching.
This one makes you build the agent loop by hand first, so you actually understand what you shipped, and only then shows you the shortcuts.
Every lesson ends with code that runs, walking you from your first API call to a sandboxed agent on Anthropic's own infrastructure.
Start here before the heavier API course below.
B-Tier: go deep
> Building with the Claude API
This is the $500 LLM-engineering bootcamp, and it costs nothing.
Eighty-four lessons and eight hours of video, from your first API call through evals, tool use, RAG, and full agent workflows.
It assumes you're comfortable in Python. Finish it and you've basically done a paid bootcamp for free.
 
> Introduction to Model Context Protocol
Out of the box, Claude can only talk. MCP is how you give it hands.
In about an hour you build servers from scratch in Python that let Claude reach your tools, your data, and your services directly, defining each tool with a simple decorator instead of fighting raw JSON.
This is the literacy behind every "Claude connected to X" demo you've ever seen.
> Introduction to Agent Skills
You teach Claude your workflow once, in a single markdown file, and it pulls that up on its own at the right moment.
Six short lessons on writing Skills, keeping them light on context, and sharing them across a team.
Small time investment, big payoff if you do the same kind of task over and over.
> Introduction to Subagents
A single Claude session gets noticeably dumber the moment it's juggling ten things at once.
Subagents fix that: fire side tasks off to helpers that work in their own clean context and report back, so your main thread stays sharp.
Four lessons, the shortest course in the catalog, and refreshingly honest about when not to bother.
> Claude Code in Action
If Claude Code 101 gets you running, this one makes you dangerous.
Fifteen lessons into the deep end: controlling context, hooks, the SDK, and wiring Claude into GitHub so it reviews your pull requests for you.
Save it for when Claude Code is already part of your daily routine.
C-Tier: your role
These all build on the 4D foundation up in S, so do that one first.
> AI Fluency for students
How to use AI to actually learn faster instead of quietly outsourcing your brain and forgetting it all by finals.
Skip it and you risk spending four years getting worse at the one thing school is supposed to teach you.
> AI Fluency for educators
Course design, assignments, and grading for a world where every student already has Claude open in another tab.
Pretending that isn't happening is the real academic dishonesty.
> AI Fluency for nonprofits
Built with GivingTuesday, for teams doing triple the work with half the staff and no budget to hire their way out of it.
> Teaching AI Fluency
The train-the-trainer one: how to teach and assess the 4Ds in other people.
Everyone teaching this right now is winging it. This is the course that isn't.
> AI Capabilities and Limitations
Fifteen minutes on how the thing actually works under the hood: next-token prediction, memory, steerability.
You stop blaming the model for what was really a bad prompt. Only bother if you're starting from zero.
D-Tier: niche
> MCP: Advanced Topics
Production MCP, the sampling and transport and scaling end of it.
Worth it only after the intro course, and only if you're shipping servers for real. Otherwise it's homework you'll never use.
> Claude with Amazon Bedrock and Vertex AI
Both are the same 85-lesson API course from above, just re-recorded for one specific cloud.
Take whichever one your company actually runs on and ignore the other. If you're not deployed on either, skip both and do the regular API course instead.
 
So, the honest skip list: if you're not on AWS or Google Cloud, the two cloud re-recordings are redundant. If you're already past "what even is AI," you can skip Capabilities and Limitations too.
Everything else earns its spot for somebody.
And yes, 16 of the 20 end with a real certificate that has your name on it. Put it on LinkedIn if you want, but the better flex is just being the person in the room who actually understands how this stuff works.

 
You don't need a $500 course. You need a free weekend and the right order.
Start with Claude 101 today. By the time most people are still deciding which paid bootcamp to buy, you'll already be the one who knows how this actually works.
The whole catalog is right here: anthropic.skilljar.com
Good luck to everyone and see you later!","preview_text":"Most people open Claude, ask it one thing, skim the reply, and close the tab.
